remember: Students will be able to recall the basic concepts of file handling, including file types, file operations, and file attributes.
Students will be able to list the different modes of opening a file for reading and writing.
Students will be able to recall the different methods for closing a file after operation.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of file streams and their importance in file handling.
Students will be able to understand the concept of file pointers and how they are used in file handling.
Students will be able to explain the difference between sequential and direct access files.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of file handling to create a program that performs file operations such as creating, reading, writing, and deleting files.
Students will be able to use file handling functions to read and write data to a file.
Students will be able to apply error handling techniques while performing file operations.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the performance of a file handling program and identify areas for improvement.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different file handling techniques and choose the best approach for a given problem.
Students will be able to evaluate the security implications of file handling operations and implement appropriate security measures.

ncert_objectives: Explain the basic concepts of file handling, including file types, file operations, and file attributes.
Describe the different modes of opening a file for reading and writing.
Explain the importance of file streams and file pointers in file handling.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the concepts of file handling, file streams, and file pointers with examples.
Write a program to create, read, write, and delete a file.
Explain the error handling techniques while performing file operations with examples.
